meta.title: Yuanzhen Zhao - Quantitative Research and Machine Learning
meta.description: Quantitative research and machine learning profile focused on high-frequency data, market microstructure, time-series prediction, and scientific computing.

hero.kicker: Quantitative Research · Machine Learning · Scientific Computing
hero.heading: I build models for noisy markets and complex systems.
hero.supporting: Trained in data-intensive science and theoretical physics, I work on high-frequency data, market microstructure, feature engineering, and time-series prediction.
hero.method: Across market data, physics, and scientific computing, the method stays consistent: define the hidden structure, test what the data can support, and build models that survive validation.

questions.title: How do market dynamics change across horizons?
questions.description: Studying intraday, daily, and cross-contract behavior in futures and equity data.

questions.title: Which signals remain stable out of sample?
questions.description: Using factor research, purged validation, ablation, and robustness checks to separate signal from noise.

questions.title: What structure transfers across domains?
questions.description: Tracing shared modelling patterns across physics, computation, and financial markets.

questions.title: How can sparse observations recover hidden state?
questions.description: Recovering latent structure from limited, noisy, or indirect measurements.

path.label: Path
path.heading: From physical principles to quantitative systems

ashare-hf-framework.title: A-share high-frequency deep learning framework
ashare-hf-framework.archiveSummary: Multi-scale sequence modelling for A-share high-frequency prediction with Mamba-3, GRU, and Transformer attention.
ashare-hf-framework.question: Can high-frequency A-share prediction combine microstructure features and multi-scale sequence models across heterogeneous horizons?
ashare-hf-framework.approach: Built Mamba-3 state-space, causal GRU, and Transformer cross-attention architectures with single-GPU and multi-GPU training, inference, logging, and model comparison.
ashare-hf-framework.finding: Created an extensible research base for testing market microstructure hypotheses beyond single-window features, with a path toward sequence, graph, and fundamental signals.

mri-super-resolution.title: Recovering anatomy from sparse MRI slices
mri-super-resolution.archiveSummary: Unpaired 3D super-resolution using a latent diffusion prior to recover fine anatomy from anisotropic MRI.
mri-super-resolution.question: Can diagnostic detail be recovered from anisotropic 3D MRI without paired low- and high-resolution training data?
mri-super-resolution.approach: Use a pre-trained 3D latent diffusion model as an anatomical prior and invert either the decoder latent or the full diffusion process.
mri-super-resolution.finding: The approach restored sharper ventricular boundaries and fine hippocampal structure than cubic interpolation, including on an unseen dataset.
mri-super-resolution.caption: Sparse input, reconstruction variants, cubic interpolation, and ground truth compared across two slice conditions.
mri-super-resolution.alt: MRI comparison showing sparse input, two diffusion-prior reconstructions, cubic interpolation, and ground truth.

antikythera-calendar.title: Finding a lunar calendar in fragmented geometry
antikythera-calendar.archiveSummary: Statistical inference on the Antikythera calendar ring using geometric error models, estimation, and HMC.
antikythera-calendar.question: Did the damaged calendar ring encode a lunar or solar calendar?
antikythera-calendar.approach: Compare isotropic and radial-tangential error models with frequentist estimation and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo.
antikythera-calendar.finding: Both frameworks favoured approximately 354 holes; the anisotropic model produced a narrower interval and supported the lunar-calendar hypothesis.
antikythera-calendar.caption: Posterior hole-count estimates shown against the 354-day lunar and 365-day solar references.
antikythera-calendar.alt: Posterior distribution for the Antikythera calendar-ring hole count with reference lines at 354 and 365.

lora-dynamics.title: LoRA fine-tuning for time-series forecasting
lora-dynamics.archiveSummary: LoRA adaptation of Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct for nonlinear multivariate forecasting under tight compute.
lora-dynamics.question: Can parameter-efficient fine-tuning adapt a compact instruction model to nonlinear multivariate time-series forecasting?
lora-dynamics.approach: Fine-tuned Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct with LoRA and converted numeric sequences into token inputs through an LLMTIME-style pipeline.
lora-dynamics.finding: Forecasting error fell by roughly 20% versus the native model, supporting PEFT-adapted LLMs for nonlinear dynamics modelling.
lora-dynamics.caption: Forecasts on held-out predator-prey trajectories compare the baseline with the LoRA-adapted model.
lora-dynamics.alt: Line plots comparing baseline and LoRA-adapted forecasts for held-out Lotka-Volterra trajectories.

medical-imaging.title: Medical Imaging Toolkit
medical-imaging.archiveSummary: PET-CT reconstruction, MRI denoising, segmentation, and radiomics assembled into one imaging workflow.

dual-autodiff.title: Dual Autodiff
dual-autodiff.archiveSummary: A tested, documented, and Cythonised forward-mode automatic-differentiation package built with dual numbers.

statistical-estimation.title: Statistical Estimation
statistical-estimation.archiveSummary: Sampling, extended likelihood, parametric bootstrapping, and sWeights applied to hidden-parameter estimation.

fluorescence-lifetimes.title: Fluorescence Lifetimes
fluorescence-lifetimes.archiveSummary: Simulation and comparison of time-correlated single-photon counting and phase fluorimetry.

quantum-optics.title: Quantum Insights into Optical Phenomena
quantum-optics.archiveSummary: Numerical links between paraxial wave optics and the Schrodinger equation.

lob-forecasting.title: WunderFund limit order book prediction
lob-forecasting.archiveSummary: Multi-horizon order-book modelling with ONNX inference; ranked #56 of 4,966 globally.
lob-forecasting.question: Can anonymised limit-order-book sequences predict future weighted volume linked to price movement?
lob-forecasting.approach: Compressed multi-level price-volume features with encoders and PCA, then ensembled LightGBM and LSTM models with ONNX CPU inference.
lob-forecasting.finding: The model reached an out-of-sample weighted IC mean near 0.19 and ranked #56 / 4,966 globally, top 1.2%.

futures-basis.title: Index futures basis dynamics
futures-basis.archiveSummary: Basis-factor research for market-neutral hedging; improved hedge-leg annual return by about 8 pp.
futures-basis.question: How should an equity market-neutral strategy select short index-futures contracts as basis seasonality and convergence change?
futures-basis.approach: Built cross-contract basis factors, term-structure features, open-interest interactions, and microstructure variables for layered backtests.
futures-basis.finding: A Ridge model under fixed short exposure improved hedge-leg annualised return by roughly 8 percentage points versus a distant quarterly roll baseline.

path.title: Quantitative Research Intern

path.summary: Researches index-futures hedge modules for equity market-neutral strategies, with emphasis on basis seasonality, convergence, and short-contract selection.
